business_type: Small photography/video studio — wedding photography primarily, business events, and Reels for local businesses
current_role: Owner-photographer and part-time editor (dislikes editing). Coordinates freelancers when needed.
weekly_tasks_text: Respond to lead inquiries on WhatsApp and Instagram DMs (frequent 'how much for a wedding?' messages); quote prices (revises pricing often); schedule shoots and discovery calls; edit photos or hand off to freelance editor; chase clients to select photos from delivered galleries; create and send invoices (sometimes late); upload galleries to gallery site; post to Instagram inconsistently; back up shoot files (sometimes forgets); travel to shoots and scout locations; general admin overhead the owner describes as 'kills me'.
current_tools_text: Instagram, WhatsApp Business, Google Drive for storage, Adobe Lightroom for photo editing, Adobe Premiere occasionally for video, Gmail, opened Notion but not actively used, Excel or Google Sheets for finances (messy), an unnamed gallery website, no CRM.
top_time_sinks_text: Editing photos and video (the biggest sink); repeating the same answers about wedding packages to new leads; clients asking 'when are my photos ready?'; finding files from old shoots across many hard drives; turning finished shoots into social posts; remembering follow-ups; general admin tasks.
error_sensitive_areas_text: Wedding dates (cannot get wrong); client names; deposits and payments; contracts when used (not always); file backups (critical because raw shoot files are irreplaceable); delivery deadlines; music or media copyright in videos.
customer_facing_areas_text: WhatsApp messages, Instagram DMs, occasional emails, price quotes, contracts, gallery delivery links, delivery-ready messages, social media posts, calls with couples and event organisers. Clients communicate in Hebrew, English, sometimes Russian.
primary_goal_text: Reduce admin chaos and increase booked weddings while looking more professional to incoming leads. Faster lead response, less repetitive admin, more consistent social content.
things_owner_refuses_to_automate_text: AI talking 'like a robot' to brides; final creative and editing-style decisions; sensitive or angry-client conversations; final price discounts; anything that risks losing wedding files; making it obvious to clients that responses are automated.
